BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow of yesterday and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent barely recognisable for beginners.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2300 m. Avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. The fresh snow of yesterday and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. Wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent barely recognisable for beginners.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2300 m. Avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m and in all aspects at elevated altitudes. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. Whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation only isolated mostly small wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2400 m. Many starting zones have released the snow.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches as before.

Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
In addition the hard wind slabs in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered in some cases still. In particular at low and intermediate altitudes and in steep rocky terrain individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities 5 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

In particular steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
In addition the hard wind slabs in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered in some cases still. In particular at low and intermediate altitudes and in steep rocky terrain individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities 5 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

In particular steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in steep terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular above approximately 2400 m: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in some place by winter sport participants. This applies in particular on very steep west, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities 5 to 15 cm of snow, and up to 20 cm in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1600 m.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in some cases be released in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Only isolated mostly small wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2400 m. Many starting zones have released the snow.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mild. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities up to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

Especially steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The old snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong melt-freeze crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities up to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

Especially steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The old snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong melt-freeze crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The wind slabs can be released easily in some places in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming wet loose snow avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The snowpack is not distinctly layered and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in some cases on very steep sunny slopes. These conditions will cause a weakening of the snowpack in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in terrain. As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation a treacherous avalanche situation will develop. This applies in particular on steep south, southwest and west facing slopes above the tree line.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The dangerous spots are found on steep slopes, especially grassy ones, as well as in areas near the ridges and at the base of rock walls, also due to the possibility of debris falling from above. As the day progresses the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes. Gliding avalanches can also occur. The wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack is moist and its surface has a crust that is not capable of bearing a load, in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mostly sunny.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily in some places in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The upper section of the snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong crust.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mild. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in some cases be released in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Only isolated mostly small wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2400 m. Many starting zones have released the snow.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: ⚠ Error: Incomplete joker sentence
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The backcountry touring conditions are generally favourable.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes, in the event of an overcast night in particular.
Slight increase in danger as a consequence of warming during the day. In particular at intermediate altitudes individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, in particular in case of releases originating from extremely steep starting zones that still retain some snow.
Weak layers exist deeper in the snowpack. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be released in deep layers and reach medium size in isolated cases. These avalanche prone locations are to be found especially at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example and in extremely steep terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The weather conditions fostered a strengthening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling here.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals; its surface consists of faceted crystals.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The backcountry and freeriding conditions are quite favourable. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be no slight decrease in the danger of moist and wet avalanches in the late morning.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The backcountry touring conditions are generally favourable.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes, in the event of an overcast night in particular.
Slight increase in danger as a consequence of warming during the day. In particular at intermediate altitudes individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, in particular in case of releases originating from extremely steep starting zones that still retain some snow.
Weak layers exist deeper in the snowpack. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be released in deep layers and reach medium size in isolated cases. These avalanche prone locations are to be found especially at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example and in extremely steep terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The weather conditions fostered a strengthening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling here.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals; its surface consists of faceted crystals.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The backcountry and freeriding conditions are quite favourable. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be no slight decrease in the danger of moist and wet avalanches in the late morning.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Above approximately 1500 m medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: A mostly favourable avalanche situation will prevail. Decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The snowpack will be in most cases well bonded. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before. As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease. In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before. Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The snowpack will be quite stable. Decrease in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the temperature drops.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on extremely steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Monday: In some localities up to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1600 m.

In particular low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.
